summary: Authoring guidance for the PPTist agentic bridge. The recommended way to build a deck from scratch: (1) pick ONE visual identity with styles.catalog -> deck.applyStyle (academic | minimal | bold | playful), then (2) for each slide pick a recipe from layouts.catalog and add it with slides.createFromLayout({ layoutId, slots }) — the engine themes, positions AND auto-fits every text box (via responsive measurement) so content never overflows. Author slot content as Markdown/plain text or structured values (arrays for bullets, { labels, series } for charts) — never hand-written HTML. Alternative: for pixel-perfect branded decks, use the built-in templates flow (deck.applyTemplate + slides.insertFromTemplate). Avoid blank slides.create / hand-placed elements unless neither a layout nor a template slide fits. Pair this with the machine schema (command payload/return types) in the same manifest.

designSystem.summary: PPTist lays elements out in a fixed pixel coordinate space, not inches. Prefer the styles + layouts flow (deck.applyStyle + slides.createFromLayout) or, for branded decks, built-in templates (deck.applyTemplate + slides.insertFromTemplate) so colors, contrast, fonts and layout are chosen for you instead of hand-picked. These tokens describe the *fallback* defaults for manual placement only.

coordinateSystem.summary: The default 16:9 canvas is 1000 x 562.5 px (viewport.size = 1000, viewport.ratio = 0.5625). Origin (0,0) is the top-left corner. Every element has left, top, width, height in px and rotate in degrees.
notes: Canvas width = viewport.size; canvas height = viewport.size * viewport.ratio. For the default 16:9 deck that is 1000 x 562.5 px.
Keep a 40px safe margin from every edge (left/top >= 40, and left+width <= 960, top+height <= 522 on the default canvas).
Coordinates are absolute px, NOT inches/points. To port an inches-based layout, multiply inches by 100 (10in wide deck -> 1000px).
rotate is clockwise degrees about the element center. Lines use start/end points instead of width/height/rotate.
When you use slides.insertFromTemplate you do NOT compute coordinates or sizes — the template slide already has designed elements. The notes above only matter for manual element placement.

For manual text, write Markdown or plain text (text.create({ markdown }) / { content } as plain text). The engine stores text as HTML internally; you should not author raw HTML tags yourself (they are escaped and show as literal text). Per-run font sizing is an advanced manual concern — reach for a layout instead.

tokens.summary: The neutral fallback palette/type scale (px) used when no template theme is applied. PREFER deck.applyTemplate (see templates.catalog) — each built-in template ships a full theme and designed slides. Override individual tokens with deck.setTheme only when a template is not enough.

styles.title: Styles
styles.summary: Curated visual-identity presets (academic, minimal, bold, playful) — each a contrast-safe color palette with named roles + a heading/body font pairing + a type scale. styles.catalog lists them; deck.applyStyle picks ONE and records theme.styleId.
styles.whenToUse: FIRST, when building a deck from scratch. Pick one style for the whole deck so every layout inherits a coherent, legible look. Use this OR the templates flow — not both.

layouts.title: Layouts
layouts.summary: Compositional slide recipes (title, section, closing, bullets, twoColumn, imageText, bigStat, quote, chart, comparison). layouts.catalog lists each layout's content slots and what they expect; slides.createFromLayout builds a themed, positioned slide from a layout id + slots.
layouts.whenToUse: After deck.applyStyle, once per slide. Pick the layout whose composition matches the slide's job and fill its slots with content. The engine themes, positions and auto-fits text to each box, so focus on clear, concise content rather than exact character counts.

templates.title: Templates
templates.summary: Built-in presentation templates (template_1 … template_8 — same as the UI template picker). templates.catalog lists them; templates.slidesCatalog lists insertable slide slugs by type; deck.applyTemplate sets the deck theme.
templates.whenToUse: ALTERNATIVE to styles + layouts, for pixel-perfect branded decks. Pick exactly one template style for the whole deck, apply its theme, then insert slides by slug. Never invent custom slide layouts when a template slide fits.

deck.title: Deck
deck.summary: Whole-document state: title, theme, viewport, templates, plus full get/set/patch.
deck.whenToUse: Apply a template theme with deck.applyTemplate up front; set the viewport once; use whole-document import/export at persistence boundaries.

slides.title: Slides
slides.summary: Insert slides from built-in templates (slides.insertFromTemplate); read, update, delete, duplicate, move, select; set background, transition and speaker remark.
slides.whenToUse: Prefer slides.insertFromTemplate (one call per slide). After insert, use text.setMarkdown on the returned placeholderElementIds/textElementIds. Avoid slides.create for deck building.

elements.title: Elements
elements.summary: Generic element CRUD plus transform, ordering, grouping, lock/hide, and shared styling (outline, shadow, fill, link).
elements.whenToUse: Use the generic create/update when you want one call across element types, or the typed sub-domains (text/shapes/charts/...) for richer helpers.

text.title: Text
text.summary: Text element create/update plus content helpers. Author content as Markdown (text.create({ markdown }) / text.setMarkdown) or plain text.
text.whenToUse: For manual slides only — layouts already place titles, body and captions. Pass Markdown or plain text; do NOT write raw HTML tags (they are escaped and show as literal text). The literal-HTML path (text.setContent / { content }) is reserved for intentionally rendering code-like markup.

richText.title: Rich text
richText.summary: Document-model edits to a text element's prose: set content, run styles and paragraph attributes.
richText.whenToUse: Use when you need paragraph alignment / inline run styling on an existing text element.

shapes.title: Shapes
shapes.summary: Vector shapes from the preset catalog (shapes.presets) or raw SVG path; fill, gradient, outline, and inner text.
shapes.whenToUse: Build dividers, callout boxes, accent bars, and backgrounds. Start from a preset, then set fill/outline/text.

lines.title: Lines
lines.summary: Straight/curved connectors with arrowheads and direction.
lines.whenToUse: Draw rules, arrows, and connectors. Lines use start/end points, not width/height.

images.title: Images
images.summary: Image element styling: source, clip/crop, radius, filters, flip, shadow, color mask, set-as-background.
images.whenToUse: Place and style raster art. Resolve media to a URL/data URL before calling the bridge.

charts.title: Charts
charts.whenToUse: Show one quantitative finding per slide. Set data with labels + series, then annotate the takeaway in text.

tables.title: Tables
tables.summary: Table create and cell/row/column edits, styling, merge/split.
tables.whenToUse: Comparison grids and small data tables. Keep them small enough to read at body size.

media.title: Media
media.summary: JSON-safe image/video/audio source assignment with mime/ext inference.
media.whenToUse: Attach already-resolved media URLs to elements.

videos.title: Videos
videos.summary: Video element source, poster, playback, autoplay, size, position.
videos.whenToUse: Embed a video clip with a poster frame.

audio.title: Audio
audio.summary: Audio element create plus source, playback, icon, transform.
audio.whenToUse: Add narration or sound; style the speaker icon.

animations.title: Animations
animations.summary: Per-slide entrance/emphasis/exit animations; list, catalog, create, reorder, trigger and duration.
animations.whenToUse: Reveal content progressively. Read animations.catalog for valid effect keys first.

notes.title: Notes
notes.summary: Slide comment threads: notes and replies.
notes.whenToUse: Author review comments; not the same as the speaker remark (slides.setRemark).

sections.title: Sections
sections.summary: Group slides into named sections for long decks.
sections.whenToUse: Add a section per major part of a long talk.

search.title: Search
search.summary: Find/replace across text, shape text and table cells.
search.whenToUse: Bulk rename or correct terminology across the deck.

history.title: History
history.summary: Commit, undo, redo against the shared history stack.
history.whenToUse: Group a multi-step edit into one undo via executeBatch, or commit/undo manually.

view.title: View
view.summary: Zoom, locale, slide navigation, enter/exit presentation.
view.whenToUse: Drive presentation playback and the active slide.

import.title: Import
import.summary: Replace the deck from a JSON-safe document (json / pptist / pptxSafe).
import.whenToUse: Load a whole deck the host already reduced to a serializable payload.

export.title: Export
export.summary: Serializable JSON snapshot of the deck.

styles.catalog.summary: List the visual-identity style presets (academic, minimal, bold, playful) with their fonts and a color preview.
styles.catalog.details: Read this first when building a deck from scratch. Each preset is a contrast-safe palette + heading/body font pairing + type scale. Pick exactly one whose tone fits the audience, then apply it with deck.applyStyle before adding any slides.
examples: const styles = controller.styles.catalog() // -> [{ id:'academic', label:'Academic', fonts:{...}, preview:{ background, title, body, accent, featureBackground } }, ...]

deck.applyStyle.summary: Apply ONE style preset as the deck's visual identity (palette, fonts, type scale). Records theme.styleId so every layout inherits it.
deck.applyStyle.details: Do this once, up front, before creating slides with slides.createFromLayout. An unknown id falls back to the default style and returns a recoverable warning — read valid ids from styles.catalog.
examples: await controller.deck.applyStyle('academic')
tips: Keep one style for the whole deck. Do not hand-pick colors or fonts on top of it — layouts read the preset's role tokens for you.

layouts.catalog.summary: List the compositional slide layouts and, for each, the content slots you fill (slot name, type, and whether it is required).
layouts.catalog.details: Call before each slide. Pick the layout id whose composition matches the slide's purpose (e.g. 'title' for the cover, 'bullets' for a list, 'twoColumn' for compare/contrast, 'bigStat' for a headline metric, 'chart' for data, 'comparison' for a table, 'closing' for the sign-off). Then pass the matching slots to slides.createFromLayout.
examples: const layouts = controller.layouts.catalog() // -> [{ id:'bullets', label:'Bulleted list', slots:[{ name:'title', type:'text', required:true }, { name:'bullets', type:'bullets', required:true }], ... }, ...]

slides.createFromLayout.summary: Build + insert a themed slide from a layout id and content slots. The PREFERRED way to add a slide when building from scratch.
slides.createFromLayout.details: The engine positions every element on the canvas, colors it from the active style preset (theme.styleId), and AUTO-FITS each text box's font size (responsive measurement) so text never overflows — you never pick coordinates, sizes, or colors. Author slot content as Markdown/plain text (titles, bullets, body) or structured values (arrays of strings for bullets; { labels, series, legends } for charts; rows for comparison). Returns the new slideId plus the created element ids.

params.name: slots
params.description: Content slots for the layout (keys + shapes per layouts.catalog).

params.name: select
params.description: Select the new slide after insert (default true).

params.name: backgroundMode
params.description: Force a feature (dark) or plain background; defaults to the layout's own preference.

examples: await controller.slides.createFromLayout({ layoutId:'title', slots:{ title:'Quarterly Review', subtitle:'Results and outlook', eyebrow:'2026 Q1' } })
await controller.slides.createFromLayout({ layoutId:'bullets', slots:{ title:'Key points', bullets:['First point','Second point','Third point'] } })
await controller.slides.createFromLayout({ layoutId:'chart', slots:{ title:'Revenue by region', chartType:'column', labels:['North','South','East'], series:[[12,28,41]] } })
tips: Apply a style once with deck.applyStyle before the first createFromLayout call.
Keep slot copy concise so the auto-fit keeps text at a comfortable size; after each slide, read the auto-screenshot to confirm the result.
Use 'title' for the first slide and 'closing' for the last; 'section' to divide a long deck.

templates.catalog.summary: List built-in presentation templates (template_1 … template_8) with human-readable names — the same catalog as the template picker UI.
templates.catalog.details: Read this first. Each entry is a full designed theme (e.g. template_1 = Crimson landscape). Pick exactly one whose tone fits the audience, then apply it with deck.applyTemplate before inserting any slides.
examples: const templates = controller.templates.catalog() // -> [{ id:'template_1', name:'Crimson landscape', ... }, ...]

deck.applyTemplate.summary: Apply ONE built-in template theme to the whole deck (fonts, colors, background).
deck.applyTemplate.details: Do this once, up front, before inserting slides. slides.insertFromTemplate also applies the theme automatically when the deck is still empty (default).

params.name: templateId
params.description: A built-in id from templates.catalog (e.g. 'template_1' for Crimson landscape).

examples: await controller.deck.applyTemplate('template_1')
tips: Keep one template style for the whole deck. Do not hand-pick colors or fonts on top of a template.

templates.slidesCatalog.summary: List insertable slides for a template, grouped by type (cover, contents, transition, content, end) with slug + description.
templates.slidesCatalog.details: Call after choosing a templateId. Each slug (e.g. cover_1, content_2) maps to one designed slide in that template. Pick the slug whose description best matches the slide you need, then call slides.insertFromTemplate.
examples: const catalog = await controller.templates.slidesCatalog('template_1')
// catalog.byType.cover -> [{ slug:'cover_1', description:'...', ... }, ...]

slides.insertFromTemplate.summary: Insert a designed slide from a built-in template by slug. The PREFERRED way to add a slide.
slides.insertFromTemplate.details: Clones the template slide (layout, decor, placeholder text boxes) into the deck. You do NOT pick coordinates or sizes. After insert, fill placeholders with text.setMarkdown on the returned placeholderElementIds (or textElementIds). Do not create new text boxes on top of an unfilled placeholder; Office-style placeholders show their prompt until that same placeholder is filled.

params.name: select
params.description: Select the new slide after insert (default true).

params.name: applyTemplateTheme
params.description: Apply template theme when deck is empty (default true).

examples: await controller.slides.insertFromTemplate({ templateId:'template_1', slug:'cover_1' })
await controller.slides.insertFromTemplate({ templateId:'template_1', slug:'content_2', index:2 })
tips: Plan slide flow: cover → contents → content slides → end. Match slug descriptions from slidesCatalog.
After insert, batch text.setMarkdown calls for each placeholderElementIds entry that should contain slide copy. Empty placeholders intentionally keep their Office-style prompt.

deck.setTheme.summary: Replace named theme properties (colors, fonts, background, outline, shadow) for the whole deck.
deck.setTheme.details: Set the design tokens once before building slides so element defaults inherit them. Accepts a partial patch; only provided keys change.

params.name: theme.themeColors
params.description: Accent palette used by charts and pickers.

examples: controller.deck.setTheme({ themeColors: ['#2E75B6','#ED7D31','#70AD47'], fontColor: '#2D2D2D', fontName: 'Arial', backgroundColor: '#FFFFFF' })
tips: Set the theme before creating elements so defaultColor/defaultFontName inherit it.

deck.setViewport.summary: Set the canvas size/aspect ratio. size is the canvas width in px; ratio is height/width.
deck.setViewport.details: Default is { size: 1000, ratio: 0.5625 } (16:9, 1000 x 562.5 px). Use ratio 0.75 for 4:3.
examples: controller.deck.setViewport({ size: 1000, ratio: 0.5625 })

slides.create.summary: Create and append a new slide; returns the created slide (use its id for element placement).
slides.create.details: Pass a partial slide. Omit id to get a generated one. background defaults to the theme. Set select:true to make it the active slide. Use slides.insert to control the position.

params.name: slide
params.description: Slide fields. elements may be provided inline, or added later with elements/text/shapes.create.

params.name: index
params.description: Insert position; appends when omitted or non-finite.

examples: const res = await controller.slides.create({ slide: { background: { type: 'solid', color: '#1F4E79' } }, select: true })
const slideId = res.data.id // reuse for every element on this slide
tips: Always read result.data.id and pass it as meta.slideId / payload.slideId for subsequent element commands.
Group the slide + its elements in one executeBatch so the whole slide is a single undo step.

slides.insert.summary: Insert a slide at a specific index (same payload as slides.create plus index).

slides.setBackground.summary: Set one slide's background (solid color, gradient, or image).
examples: controller.slides.setBackground(slideId, { type: 'solid', color: '#1F4E79' })

slides.setRemark.summary: Set the speaker notes (remark) for a slide. Content is HTML.
examples:
controller.slides.setRemark(slideId, '

Pause here and ask the audience.

')

elements.create.summary: Create any element type on a slide (manual placement). payload.element.type is required.
elements.create.details: Generic creator across all element types, for bespoke slides a template can't express. Prefer slides.insertFromTemplate for whole slides, or the typed creators (text.create, shapes.create, charts.create, tables.create) for single elements. Provide left/top/width/height in canvas px and rotate in degrees. For text, pass Markdown via text.create instead of hand-writing HTML here.

params.name: slideId
params.description: Target slide; defaults to the current slide.

examples:
controller.elements.create({ slideId, element: { type:'text', left:40, top:40, width:920, height:90, rotate:0, content:'

Action title

', defaultFontName:'Arial', defaultColor:'#1F4E79' } })
tips: Text font size lives in inline HTML styles inside content, not in a fontSize field.

text.create.summary: Create a text element (manual placement). Author content as Markdown via `markdown` (recommended) or plain text via `content`.
text.create.details: Pass `markdown` for formatted copy (the bridge converts it to the HTML PPTist stores). `content` is treated as literal markup — pass plain text there, or intentional HTML only when you specifically want to render markup (e.g. a code snippet). Do NOT type raw HTML tags into `markdown`: they are escaped and appear as literal text (the bridge warns when it detects this). The fallback color/font come from defaultColor/defaultFontName.

params.name: content
params.description: Literal content (plain text, or intentional HTML for code-like markup). Wins over markdown.

params.name: element
params.description: Geometry/styling: left, top, width, height, rotate, defaultColor, defaultFontName, lineHeight, wordSpace, fill.

examples: controller.text.create({ slideId, markdown: '**Key finding:** treated group improved 18%', element: { left:520, top:130, width:440, height:300, rotate:0, defaultColor:'#2D2D2D' } })
controller.text.create({ slideId, markdown: '- First point\n- Second point\n- Third point', element:{ left:40, top:140, width:920, height:300, rotate:0 } })
tips: Prefer slides.insertFromTemplate for whole slides. Markdown flavor: markdown-it + markdown-it-texmath (KaTeX). Write Markdown, not HTML.

text.setMarkdown.summary: Replace a text element's content from a Markdown string (converted to HTML).
text.setMarkdown.details: Author in Markdown. Raw HTML tags are escaped (they would render as literal text) and trigger a warning — use Markdown syntax instead.
examples: controller.text.setMarkdown(elementId, '- First point\n- Second point\n- Third point')

text.setContent.summary: Replace a text element's content with a literal string (the literal-markup path).
text.setContent.details: Use for plain text, or for intentionally rendering HTML markup (e.g. a code snippet). For normal formatted copy use text.setMarkdown instead.

shapes.create.summary: Create a shape. Start from a preset (shapes.presets) or supply a raw SVG path + viewBox.
shapes.create.details: Use shapes for accent rules, callout boxes, dividers and color blocks. Provide left/top/width/height in px; fill is a hex color; outline sets a border; text adds centered inner HTML text. Read shapes.presets(categoryKey?) for ready-made path/viewBox pairs.

params.name: element
params.description: viewBox, path, fill, outline, gradient, opacity, text, plus geometry.

examples:
// thin accent rule under a title
controller.shapes.create({ slideId, element: { left:40, top:120, width:920, height:3, viewBox:[200,200], path:'M 0 0 L 200 0 L 200 200 L 0 200 Z', fill:'#CCCCCC' } })
tips: For a filled background band, place a wide low-height shape behind text with a lower z-order.

shapes.presets.summary: List preset shapes (path + viewBox) grouped by category. Pass a category key to filter.
examples: controller.shapes.presets() // all categories
controller.shapes.presets('rect')

charts.create.summary: Create a chart element with type + data (labels, legends, series).
charts.create.details: One chart per slide. Provide labels (category axis), legends (series names) and series (arrays of numbers). State the takeaway in an adjacent text element rather than relying on the chart alone.

params.name: chartType
params.description: bar, column, line, pie, ring, area, radar, scatter.

params.name: data
params.description: { labels: string[], legends: string[], series: number[][] }.

examples: controller.charts.create({ slideId, chartType:'bar', data:{ labels:['Q1','Q2','Q3','Q4'], legends:['Effect %'], series:[[28,22,15,8]] }, element:{ left:40, top:130, width:540, height:360, rotate:0 } })
tips: Put the interpretive text on the right (~520px) and the chart on the left (~40px).

tables.create.summary: Create a table with rows x columns of cells.
tables.create.details: Supply data as a 2D array of cells. Edit cells with tables.setCell, style with tables.setCellStyle, and grow/shrink with insert/delete row/column.
examples: controller.tables.create({ slideId, element:{ left:40, top:140, width:920, height:300, rotate:0, colWidths:[0.5,0.5], data:[[{ text:'Metric' },{ text:'Value' }],[{ text:'N' },{ text:'2,400' }]] } })

animations.create.summary: Add an animation to an element on a slide. Read animations.catalog for valid effect keys.
animations.create.details: type is 'in' (entrance), 'out' (exit) or 'attention' (emphasis). effect must be a key from animations.catalog. trigger is 'click', 'meantime' or 'auto'.
examples: const cat = controller.animations.catalog()
controller.animations.create(slideId, { elId: elementId, type:'in', effect:'fadeIn', trigger:'click', duration:600 })

executeBatch.summary: Run several commands as one atomic, single-undo step. Prefer this when one instruction expands to many edits.
executeBatch.details: Atomic by default: any failure rolls back the whole batch. Pass { atomic:false } to keep successful child changes. Use deterministic ids so a failed child can be located.

guides.title: How to build a deck (styles + layouts)
guides.summary: The recommended flow for a fresh deck: pick one style, then build each slide from a layout by filling its content slots.
body: 1. Read styles.catalog and pick exactly ONE visual identity (academic, minimal, bold, playful) that fits the audience and topic.

2. Apply it once: deck.applyStyle({ styleId:'academic' }). This sets the deck theme and records theme.styleId; every layout then inherits its palette, fonts and type scale. Do not hand-pick colors or fonts after this.
3. Plan the slide flow (e.g. title -> section / bullets / chart / ... -> closing). Keep one todo per slide.
4. Read layouts.catalog and, for each slide, pick the layout id whose composition matches the slide's job. Note each slot's name, type and whether it is required.

5. Add the slide with slides.createFromLayout({ layoutId, slots }). Fill slots with Markdown/plain text (titles, bullets, body) or structured values (arrays for bullets; { labels, series } for charts; rows for comparison). The engine themes, positions and auto-fits text to every box — you never set coordinates, colors, or font sizes.

6. After each slide, look at the auto-screenshot to confirm the result. Text auto-fits its frame so it won't overflow; if the auto-fit made a box shrink uncomfortably small, that slide is overloaded — split it or trim the copy and rebuild.
7. Use 'title' for the cover and 'closing' for the sign-off; 'section' to divide a long deck.
8. Only drop to the templates flow (deck.applyTemplate + slides.insertFromTemplate) for pixel-perfect branded decks, or manual slides.create when neither a layout nor a template slide fits.

guides.title: How to build a deck (alternative: built-in templates)
guides.summary: The branded-deck alternative to styles + layouts: pick one template style, apply its theme, insert slides by slug, then edit text.
body: 1. Read templates.catalog and pick exactly ONE template (template_1 … template_8) whose tone fits the audience (e.g. template_1 Crimson landscape for bold keynotes, template_5 Minimal green for crisp, understated decks).
2. Apply it once: deck.applyTemplate({ templateId:'template_1' }). This sets the deck theme. Do not hand-pick colors or fonts after this.
3. Read templates.slidesCatalog({ templateId }) — slides are grouped by type: cover, contents, transition, content, end. Each entry has a slug and description.

4. For each slide in your outline, call slides.insertFromTemplate({ templateId, slug }) with the slug that best matches (e.g. cover_1 for title, contents_1 for agenda, content_2 for a text+image slide).

5. Use the returned placeholderElementIds/textElementIds: batch text.setMarkdown (or text.update) to replace placeholder copy. Do not add separate text boxes over placeholders. Author Markdown/plain text only — never HTML.
6. After creating or editing a slide, look at the auto-screenshot. Fix overflow and literal markup before moving on.
7. Only use slides.create + manual elements when no template slide in the catalog fits.

guides.title: Authoring content (Markdown / plain text / structured data — not HTML)
guides.summary: What to put in text and layout slots, and why raw HTML does not belong there.
body: Layout slots and the markdown text path accept Markdown, plain text, or structured values (arrays for bullets; { labels, series } for charts; rows for comparison). The engine renders them into clean, themed HTML for you.

Do NOT write raw HTML tags (
, ,
, , ...) into Markdown/plain-text inputs. They are ESCAPED and appear as literal angle-bracket text on the slide. The bridge attaches a warning when it detects this — treat that warning as a must-fix.
Use Markdown syntax for emphasis: **bold**, _italic_, `code`, and - / 1. for lists. Math uses $...$ / $$...$$ (KaTeX).

The only place literal HTML is appropriate is the explicit literal path (text.setContent / element.content) when you intentionally want to render markup — for example, displaying an HTML code snippet as content. Even then, prefer a fenced code block in Markdown when you just want code to look like code.
If a screenshot shows literal tags or markup characters on a slide, you used the wrong path: re-author the text as Markdown via text.setMarkdown.

guides.title: Manual placement (fallback)
guides.summary: For bespoke slides a layout can't express: the read -> style -> per-slide batch -> review loop.
body: Prefer built-in templates (see the templates-flow guide). Use manual placement only when no template slide fits.
1. Read the current deck (controller.export.json() or deck.get) and apply a template theme (deck.applyTemplate) so element defaults inherit a coherent palette and font.
2. For each slide: create the slide, capture result.data.id, then add its elements in a single executeBatch so the slide is one undo step.
3. Place elements in canvas px (default 1000 x 562.5). Keep a 40px margin and a clear visual hierarchy: title row, content area, footer/citation.
4. Author body text in Markdown (text.create({ markdown }) / text.setMarkdown), never raw HTML.
5. Screenshot the slide and fix overflow/contrast/alignment before moving on. Use meta.dryRun to validate a risky payload before applying it.

guides.title: Coordinate system & sizing
guides.summary: Everything is absolute pixels on a 1000 x 562.5 canvas.
body: Canvas = viewport.size wide by viewport.size * viewport.ratio tall. Default 16:9 -> 1000 x 562.5 px. Origin is top-left.
Element box: left, top, width, height (px) and rotate (clockwise degrees about the center). Lines use start/end points instead.
Safe area: keep left/top >= 40 and right/bottom edges <= 960 / 522 on the default canvas.
Porting an inches layout (e.g. pptxgenjs 10in x 5.625in): multiply inches by 100. 0.5in margin -> 50px; a 5.4in-wide chart -> 540px.
Text size is set by inline HTML styles inside content, e.g. <span style="font-size:40px">. defaultColor/defaultFontName are only fallbacks for unsized runs.

guides.title: Title slide
guides.summary: Dark background, large statement title, subtitle, author/affiliation, accent rule.
body: Slide: background solid primary (#1F4E79).
Title text: left 70, top 150, width 860, height 180. content uses ~64px bold white, framed as a statement or question.
Subtitle text: left 70, top 330, width 860, height 40. ~18px in a light tint (#A0BBDD).
Author/affiliation text: left 70, top 380, width 860, height 60. ~16px (#CADCFC).
Accent rule: a shape at left 70, top 360, width 200, height 4, fill accent (#2E75B6).
Avoid: clip art, logos (unless requested), decorative images.

guides.title: Section divider
guides.summary: Orient the audience at the start of each major part of a long deck.
body: Slide: dark background (a slightly lighter navy, e.g. #1A3A5C, for variety).
Eyebrow text: 'Part 2' at left 40, top 180, ~18px (#7BAFD4).
Section title text: left 40, top 220, width 920, ~40px bold white.
Accent rule: shape left 40, top 330, width 250, height 6, fill accent.

guides.title: Content slide (action title + bullets)
guides.summary: An action title that states the takeaway, a thin divider, and concise body bullets.
body: Action title text: left 40, top 30, width 920, height 90. ~40px bold primary. Write a full sentence that states the point, not just a topic.
Divider: shape left 40, top 120, width 920, height 3, fill rule (#CCCCCC).
Body text: left 40, top 140, width 920, height 360. Author with Markdown bullets (text.create({ markdown })). Keep <= ~40 words; body >= 24px.
Optional citation: text left 40, top 520, ~14px muted.

guides.title: Two-column layout
guides.summary: A header per column with aligned body blocks; good for design vs. outcomes.
body: Left column header: text left 40, top 130, width 440, ~30px accent bold.
Left body: text left 40, top 175, width 440, height 300, Markdown bullets.
Right column header: text left 520, top 130, width 440, ~30px accent bold.
Right body: text left 520, top 175, width 440, height 300, Markdown bullets.
Keep the same top for both columns so headers align.

guides.title: Results slide (chart + interpretation)
guides.summary: One exhibit, one finding. Chart left, interpretive text right, takeaway in the title.
body: Action title text: left 40, top 30, width 920, height 90. State the result (e.g. 'Treated group earns 18% more').
Divider: shape left 40, top 120, width 920, height 3, fill rule.
Chart: charts.create at left 40, top 135, width 540, height 360 with labels + one series.
Interpretation header: text left 620, top 135, width 330, ~30px accent bold.
Interpretation bullets: text left 620, top 180, width 330, height 250, Markdown.
Source citation: text left 40, top 520, ~14px muted.
Annotate the single key number directly with a small highlight shape (#FFF2CC) + label over the chart.

guides.title: Conclusions slide
guides.summary: Mirror the title slide; numbered takeaways that stay on screen during Q&A.
body: Slide: dark background primary (matches the title slide for a 'sandwich' structure).
Eyebrow text: 'Conclusions' at left 40, top 25, ~20px light tint.
Accent rule: shape left 40, top 70, width 920, height 4, fill accent.
Takeaways text: left 40, top 90, width 920, height 350. Numbered Markdown list, ~26px white, with the lead clause of each point bold.
Contact/link text: left 40, top 480, ~14px light tint.

guides.title: Working with shapes
guides.summary: How to build dividers, rules, callout boxes and color blocks from presets or raw paths.
body: Start from a preset: const presets = controller.shapes.presets('rect'); then spread one into shapes.create and override geometry/fill.
Raw path: provide viewBox (e.g. [200,200]) and an SVG path in that box; PPTist scales it to width/height. A filled rectangle path is 'M 0 0 L 200 0 L 200 200 L 0 200 Z'.
Accent rule / divider: a wide, low-height filled rectangle (height 3-6px) in the rule or accent color.
Callout box: a rounded-rect preset with a light fill (#EBF3FA or #FFF2CC) and a 1.5-2px outline in accent; add centered inner text via element.text or shapes.setText.
Background band: a full-width shape with a low z-order (send it to back with elements.sendToBack) sitting behind text.
Use opacity and gradient (shapes.setGradient) sparingly for depth; keep contrast high enough to read body text.

guides.title: Authoring text in Markdown
guides.summary: The bridge converts Markdown to the HTML PPTist stores. Use it for fast, readable copy.
body: Flavor: markdown-it + markdown-it-texmath (KaTeX).
Use text.create({ markdown }) / text.setMarkdown(elementId, markdown), or await controller.markdownToHtml(md).

guides.title: Pre-delivery checklist
guides.summary: Quick checks before declaring a deck done.
body: Every content slide has an action title that states the takeaway (a full sentence, not a topic).
Reading the titles alone tells the complete argument.
Results slides show one exhibit each, with the key number annotated.
Body text >= 24px; no slide has more than ~40 words of body text.
Borrowed figures/data carry an in-slide citation.
A 40px margin is respected on every edge; nothing is clipped off-canvas.
Title and conclusions slides share the dark treatment (sandwich structure).
